text: TDD (Kent Beck, 2002) is a development practice: write a failing test → write minimum code to pass → refactor. Red-Green-Refactor cycle. TDD ensures testability, drives design toward small, loosely coupled units, and provides a regression safety net. Not about testing — it's about design.
Benefits: 40-80% fewer bugs in production (IBM/Microsoft studies), confidence to refactor, living documentation.
